summary.en: Denver's creative pulse, an industrial-chic landscape where old brick warehouses have been reborn as canvases for ambitious street art, buzzing with the energy of artists, brewers, and chefs.
Denver's funky, non-conformist soul, this eclectic stretch is a treasure trove of vintage boutiques, antique emporiums, indie music venues, and unpretentious dive bars with a lot of history.
Perched on a bluff across from downtown, LoHi is a trendy, scenic neighborhood of contrasts, where sharp, modern architecture sits next to restored Victorian homes, with unbeatable skyline views.
Stately, historic, and a little bit weird, this is one of Denver's oldest neighborhoods, defined by grand 19th-century mansions, tree-lined parkways, and a bohemian undercurrent.

Denver's epicenter of Latin culture and creativity, this vibrant, colorful corridor is packed with art galleries and studios, proudly displaying its Hispanic roots through stunning murals and authentic cuisine.
A charming, laid-back main street that feels like a small town in the city, Tennyson is highly walkable and fiercely local, lined with independent boutiques, cozy restaurants, and historic theaters.

description.en: An intimate, no-frills taproom that feels like a friend's cool, beer-making garage, beloved by locals for its well-crafted, experimental brews.
Located inside The Ramble Hotel, this world-class cocktail bar offers meticulously crafted drinks in a moody, sophisticated space inspired by 17th-century French salons.
A unique walk-up window serving high-quality draft cocktails in Denver's first "common consumption area," perfect for a pre-show drink.
A temple to pozole and mezcal, this spot serves unapologetically authentic and deeply flavorful food, earning it a reputation as one of the city's best Mexican restaurants.
A social enterprise providing culinary training to immigrant and refugee women, serving outstanding fast-casual Mexican breakfast and lunch that supports a great mission.
A bustling, upscale food hall perfect for groups with diverse tastes, offering everything from pizza and oysters to gourmet ice cream.
The heart of RiNo, these alleys between Larimer and Walnut are a constantly evolving outdoor gallery of murals from local and international artists.
An unexpectedly sleek spot pairing sophisticated cocktails with full-motion Formula 1 racing simulators for a unique entertainment experience.
A cozy, plant-filled vegan bar where inventive cocktails and a curated liquor list are celebrated, perfect for thoughtful conversation.
A vibrant, vegetable-focused restaurant proving vegan food can be soulful and satisfying, with handmade pastas as a highlight.
A Denver institution serving one of the city's most beloved burgers in a classic, neighborhood-joint setting just off South Broadway.
A dense concentration of antique shops where you can hunt for everything from mid-century modern furniture to Victorian oddities.
A standout vintage clothing store packed with unique and high-quality finds from various decades.
A gem in the Baker district known for its well-curated collection of clothing and accessories, offering a boutique-like vintage experience.
A chaotic and wonderful mash-up of a used bookstore, coffee shop, comic book store, and pinball arcade where you can lose hours.
A modern food hall built from shipping containers, whose main attraction is a sprawling rooftop bar with one of the best panoramic views of the city.
Hidden behind a bookshelf in a corner bookstore, this is one of Denver's original and best speakeasies, with serious cocktails and an intimate atmosphere.
A vibrant Mexican spot where the food is as compelling as the extensive mezcal list, with a tostada that could 'alter your life's trajectory'.
A bright and cheerful breakfast-only restaurant serving inventive, chef-driven takes on morning classics, like their 'animal style' hash browns.
Worth the short walk from LoHi, this ramen-focused spot is known for its rich broth and what many call the best kimchi dumplings in town.
An architectural landmark housed in a 28-foot-tall vintage milk can, this whimsical and delicious neighborhood hub is an essential Denver experience.
This iconic white pedestrian bridge connects LoHi to downtown over I-25, offering a fantastic vantage point for photos of the skyline.
Step off the main drags to wander residential streets and find a beautiful mix of Denver's architectural history, from Queen Anne homes to classic brick bungalows.
A beloved vegetarian institution that has been a neighborhood anchor for over two decades, functioning as a coffee shop, restaurant, and full bar.
A stylish and comfortable coffee shop by day and a sophisticated cocktail and wine bar by night, with great mid-century modern decor.
A simple, focused sandwich shop that does one thing exceptionally well: porchetta. A must-try for a delicious and satisfying lunch.
Located just on the northern edge of Capitol Hill, this spot is famous for unique Asian-inspired comfort food, especially the bacon fried rice.
A hidden architectural gem featuring a stunning collection of 1930s Art Deco apartment buildings, each named for a different literary icon.
A free, accessible gallery of contemporary art where you can wander the lobby and common areas to see an impressive, professionally curated collection.
On the west-facing steps of the Capitol, find the engraved marker designating the exact point that is 5,280 feet above sea level—a classic Denver photo op.
A unique hi-fi listening bar for the discerning music and wine lover, focused on listening to full vinyl albums on a high-end sound system.
The cocktail bar inside Meow Wolf is an attraction in its own right, with drinks as creative and otherworldly as the art installation.
A legendary, no-frills taqueria that is a rite of passage for any Denver food lover. The green chile-smothered breakfast burrito is a mandatory experience.
The main purpose of the neighborhood. Spend an afternoon popping into numerous independent galleries showcasing everything from contemporary painting to traditional folk art.
While recently relocated, this foundational Chicano arts institution continues to celebrate the neighborhood's cultural heritage through events and exhibitions.
A beloved neighborhood hub famous for its artisanal pizza and enormous, constantly rotating craft beer list in a casual, friendly vibe.
A short walk from Tennyson, this brewery's main draw is its rooftop patio with stunning, unobstructed views of Sloan's Lake and the downtown skyline.

name.en: Denver Biscuit Co. at Atomic Cowboy

description.en: This location serves the same legendary, oversized biscuit sandwiches that have become a decadent and essential Denver brunch experience.
Possibly Denver's most charming boutique, specializing in artisanal goods including a unique line of all-natural, floral-infused crackers and salts.
A historic 1927 movie palace, now a live music venue, whose iconic and beautifully maintained marquee is the landmark that anchors the entire street.
At the north end of the shopping district, this lovely park offers a peaceful escape with a walking path around the lake.
A delightful independent bookstore and wine bar where you can browse curated shelves with a glass of wine in hand.

prompt.en: Capture a photo that perfectly blends the neighborhood's industrial past with its creative present. Look for old brick, metal beams, and modern art in a single frame.
Take a selfie where you or a friend appear to be interacting with a character or element in a large street mural.
Find and photograph a small, hidden piece of art—a sticker, a tiny sculpture, or a painted utility box—that most people would walk right past.
Find a mural with a vibrant color palette and take a photo focusing only on the abstract combination of colors.
Capture the atmosphere inside a local brewery or taproom. Focus on the community feel, not just the beer.
Get up close to a mural and take a detailed shot that shows the texture of the spray paint on the brick or concrete wall.
Keep an eye out for artists creating new work. If you spot one, respectfully capture a photo of the creative process in action.
Take a photo of a delicious-looking dish from one of the area's food halls, making it look like a magazine cover.
Photograph the exterior of a building where you can clearly see how an old warehouse has been converted into a modern gallery, shop, or restaurant.
Go into one of the art alleys and take a low-angle shot looking up to make the murals feel massive and immersive.
Find a puddle or a window and capture the reflection of a colorful mural.
Take an artistic photo of your craft beer or cocktail, focusing on the color and light.
Capture the interesting shadows cast by fire escapes, signs, or buildings on a large mural.
The rhinoceros is the unofficial mascot of RiNo. Find a depiction of a rhino in a mural or sculpture and take its picture.
Take a selfie with a friend in front of a mural that has two distinct characters, with each of you mimicking one of them.
Capture a photo of a storefront or sign that perfectly encapsulates the funky, non-conformist soul of South Broadway.
Photograph the most bizarre or out-of-place item you can find for sale in an antique shop window.
Capture the reflection of modern street life (bikes, people, cars) in the window of an old vintage or antique store.
Take a selfie that shows off the neighborhood's independent spirit. Pose in front of an indie music venue, a quirky shop, or a dive bar.
Photograph a detail that shows the neighborhood's history and grit—peeling paint on an old sign, a worn-down doorway, or a historic brick pattern.
Find a used bookstore and take a photo that captures the feeling of being surrounded by stacks and stacks of books.
Find a cool, old-school neon sign and capture its glow, especially at dusk.
Take a flat-lay photo of a cool vintage item you find, or try something on and take a picture of the unique pattern or fabric.
Take a candid photo of a person who you think embodies the unique character of the Baker neighborhood.
Photograph your meal at a local diner or burger joint, making it look like the ultimate comfort food.
Find a shop window with a wild collection of unrelated items and take a photo that highlights the strange combination.
Look for a 'ghost sign'—an old, faded advertisement painted on the side of a brick building—and take a picture of it.
Find a pinball machine or an old arcade game and take a dynamic action shot of it being played.
Find a cozy bar or cafe filled with plants and take a photo that captures the lush, green atmosphere.
Find a record store and take a selfie holding up an album with the most amazing or ridiculous cover art you can find.
Find a spot with a great view of the downtown Denver skyline and capture a panoramic photo.
Take a photo that frames a sharp, modern building right next to a beautifully restored Victorian home.
Capture the scene on a bustling rooftop patio, showing people enjoying drinks with the city in the background.
Get a scoop of ice cream or another sweet treat and take a happy selfie with it.
Find an old Victorian house and take a close-up shot of a unique architectural detail, like gingerbread trim, a stained-glass window, or ornate ironwork.
Go to a pedestrian bridge and take a photo that uses the lines of the bridge to frame the city skyline.
Take a 'cheers' photo with a friend, clinking glasses with a scenic view in the background.
Find a new, modern building and take an abstract photo focusing on its sharp angles, glass, and geometric shapes.
Find a bar or restaurant with a hidden or unassuming entrance and take a mysterious photo of the doorway.
Capture an overhead shot of a beautiful brunch spread at one of the neighborhood's trendy spots.
At dusk, take a selfie with the glowing lights of the downtown skyline behind you.
Wander off the main roads and take a photo down a quiet, tree-lined residential street.
Find an outdoor staircase and use it as a leading line in a photo pointing towards a great view.
This is a very dog-friendly area. Take a cute picture of a dog enjoying the neighborhood.
The Little Man Ice Cream milk can is an icon. Take a creative photo that uses forced perspective to make it look like you're holding it or drinking from it.
Find a grand, historic mansion and take a photo that captures its stately presence. Try a low angle to make it look even more imposing.
Take a dignified, serious-looking selfie in front of the Colorado State Capitol building or another grand, historic structure.
Find an Art Deco building and take a close-up, artistic shot of its unique typography, geometric patterns, or facade carvings.
Capture a scene that represents the neighborhood's bohemian undercurrent—a quirky coffee shop, a unique storefront, or an artistic detail.
Photograph the long shadow of an old tree falling across the brick facade of a 19th-century mansion.
Find one of the 'One Mile Above Sea Level' markers on the Capitol steps and take a photo of your foot next to it.
The neighborhood is described as 'a little bit weird.' Find and photograph something that fits this description perfectly.
Look up! Find a unique gargoyle, carving, or other interesting architectural detail on the roofline of an old building.
Take a photo inside a cozy coffee shop that feels like a perfect all-day hangout spot.
Capture a beautiful shot down one of the neighborhood's signature tree-lined streets or parkways.
The Art Hotel isn't the only place with art. Find a piece of public art or a sculpture and photograph it.
Find an ornate, historic doorway and take a photo that makes you wonder who has passed through it over the last century.
Find a bar or cafe with mid-century modern decor and take a stylish photo of the interior.
The area is known for ghost stories. Take a slightly spooky, atmospheric photo of a mansion that looks like it could be haunted.
On Poet's Row, find the building named after your favorite author (or any author!) and take a selfie with their name.
Capture a single photo that is exploding with the vibrant colors that define the Art District on Santa Fe.
Find a mural or piece of public art that clearly celebrates the neighborhood's Hispanic roots and photograph it.
Take a photo looking into an art gallery from the street, capturing the art and the atmosphere inside.
Find a colorful mural and take a selfie while wearing or holding something that matches one of its dominant colors.
Get some authentic Mexican food and take a mouth-watering close-up shot. Make it look irresistible!
Find a piece of art that isn't a mural—look for sculptures, decorated benches, or artistic bike racks.
Walk a block and see how many calaveras (sugar skulls) you can spot in murals, sculptures, or shop windows. Photograph your favorite one.
Take a photo that captures the palpable sense of community in the neighborhood.
Near the entrance to Meow Wolf, take a selfie that looks as weird and mind-bending as the attraction itself.
Many artists have studios here. Peek through a window and (respectfully) capture a glimpse of an artist's creative space.
The neighborhood has a rich history of lowrider culture. Keep an eye out for a classic car and snap a photo if you see one.
Find a restaurant with a beautiful, colorful patio and take a photo that makes it look like the perfect place to spend an afternoon.
Photograph a shop or gallery sign that is a work of art in itself.
Find a dish smothered in green chile and take a photo before you dig in. Bonus points if it's a breakfast burrito.
Find a hi-fi listening bar and capture the moody, relaxed vibe of enjoying music and a drink.
Take a photo looking down Tennyson Street that captures its charming, laid-back, 'small town in the city' vibe.
Capture a beautiful shot of the historic marquee of The Oriental Theater, the landmark that anchors the street.
Photograph an independent boutique or shopfront that looks fiercely local and unique.
Take a selfie of yourself looking relaxed while enjoying a coffee, a beer, or a book at a local spot.
Get a slice of artisanal pizza or a craft beer and take an artistic, top-down photo of it.
Go to Berkeley Lake Park and take a peaceful photo of the lake with the trees and sky reflected in the water.
Find a bookstore that also serves drinks. Capture the perfect combination of a book and a glass of wine or coffee.
Find a charming boutique and photograph a unique item in the window display.
Photograph a colorful and healthy-looking plant-based dish from a local restaurant.
Capture a photo of people enjoying one of the many pleasant patios along the street.
Find a product—food, drink, or otherwise—that has been infused with flowers and take a beautiful photo of it.
If you get a biscuit sandwich, take a photo using a low angle to make it look like a giant, delicious mountain.
Take a photo of an interesting or quirky window display at one of the independent shops.
Capture a candid shot of people casually strolling and browsing along the walkable main street.
Find a spot with a view of Sloan's Lake and the downtown skyline and take a picture that captures the beautiful, unobstructed view.
